intro: Simira longifolia, also known as Keura or Australian Mahogany, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical rainforests of Northern Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities, as well as its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
